title: Central, coastal A.P. receive season’s first spell of heavy rainfall
content: NTR district records widespread and very heavy rainfall during the 24 hours ending Thursday morning, while the neighbouring districts of Krishna, Guntur, Bapatla also receives widespread heavy rainfall

title: Two held for luring job seekers into cyber scam network
content: The Cyber Crime Wing issued an advisory to the public asking them to verify all overseas job offers only through authorised recruitment agencies and official government channels

title: Tinkering laboratory at school campus in Ranipet inaugurated
content: The laboratory will serve as a valuable platform and offer meaningful exposure to students not only within the school campus but also to those in the district

title: Farmer’s house near Chengam burgled
content: The police said that on their return from a temple festival, the family noticed damage to the main door of the house and valuables missing

title: CM’s promise on vehicle modification in Kerala raises many doubts
content: V.D. Satheesan’s recent assurance that the State govt. will permit ‘non-dangerous’ vehicle modifications was warmly welcomed by automobile enthusiasts. However, when the MVD came up with a list of permitted modifications, there was disappointment all around. A reality check on the legal and practical aspects of the proposal

title: Major reshuffle in police force in Kerala; P. Vijayan is ADGP (Law and Order)
content: S. Sreejith, Additional Director General of Police, Headquarters, has been transferred and posted as ADGP (Prisons and Correctional Services)

title: Bengaluru with heavy migrant influx needs greater Central aid, says Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar at NITI Aayog meet
content: Karnataka Chief Minister also suggests strengthening urban and regional economic growth centres to generate quality employment opportunities beyond Bengaluru

title: From cotton to chilli and now mango, a farmer-breeder’s long harvest
content: Kongara Ramesh has cultivated around 100 varieties of mango and is screening 15 to 20 that show particularly promising traits across nutrition, flavour, storability, and appearance

title: Govt alerts maritime stakeholders after missile strike on tanker kills three Indians
content: Authorities have directed all maritime stakeholders to remain on heightened alert and strictly adhere to security protocols while operating in conflict-prone waters.

title: BJP wins two Rajya Sabha seats in Rajasthan; Congress gets one
content: With the unopposed election of three candidates, Rajasthan has now an equal representation of five Rajya Sabha MPs each from the ruling BJP and the Opposition Congress

title: Rajya Sabha polls: All three BJP candidates from Madhya Pradesh elected unopposed
content: ECI yet to take a call on Congress challenge against returning officer’s rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan’s nomination; Congress says rejection was “unconstitutional”, accuses BJP of “seat theft”

title: Medical practice simplified in Andhra Pradesh
content: Those with recognised medical qualification and holding valid registration can practice in the State without the need for obtaining separate registration from the AP Medical Council or NOC from the State Medical Council or Union Territory Medical Council where they are registered

title: Syringe recall at AIIMS was a precautionary, patient-safety measure: Health Ministry
content: In a letter written to Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda, Rajya Sabha MP Haris Beeran had said that in the past month two separate recall notices were issued for disposable 10 ml syringes with needles supplied by different manufacturers, citing quality-related complaints

title: Modi surrendered India's sovereignty to Trump: Kejriwal
content: ‘PM Modi is saying ‘thank you’ to Trump. Thank you for exactly what? For attacking ships with Indians on board and killing Indian nationals?’ AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal posted on X

title: Businesses along Bengaluru-Mysuru highway fear impact of proposed decentralised tolling system, new entry/exit points
content: The NHAI is planning to establish toll collection points at various entry and exit locations along the 119-km highway, replacing the existing system under which vehicles pay a fixed toll at designated toll plazas.

title: Three NDA candidates are elected unopposed to Rajya Sabha from northeast
content: The BJP’s Tai Tagak and Adhikarimayum Sharda Devi were elected from Arunachal Pradesh and Manipur; the NPP’s James P.K. Sangma was elected from Meghalaya

title: APSRTC JAC demands cancellation of e-bus tenders awarded to private firms
content: The other major demands include filling 10,000 vacant posts, purchasing 4,000 new buses, resolving issues arising from Stree Shakti scheme, clearing pending PRC and DA arrears and immediate constitution of 12th PRC

title: Indian Navy safely recovers unexploded missile warhead from oil tanker bound for Kochi
content: The ordnance recovered from MT Olympic Life has since been transported to a secure facility for safe storage and detailed examination, the Navy said

title: Monsoon 2026 updates: India expected to receive below-average rain over next two weeks, report says
content: IMD issues 'red' alert for thunderstorms, lightning, gusty winds in Delhi for a few hours through June 11 night

title: France backs ‘Make in India’ in defence, signals new model for Rafale deal ahead of Modi visit
content: French diplomatic sources say future defence cooperation with India will be aligned with the ‘Make in India’ initiative and based on an equal partnership. They express optimism about the prospects of enhanced cooperation in the nuclear sector, citing recent legislative reforms in India.

title: Fans brace for sleepless World Cup madness
content: Supporters of several football giants are preparing for late-night and early-morning kick-offs as the World Cup gets under way

title: U.S. attacks third ship with Indian seafarers; crew safe
content: External Affairs Ministry says ‘deeply worried’ by attacks but blames conflict in the region; Congress asks Prime Minister Modi to take responsibility while CPI(M) urges strong condemnation by government

title: Cockroach Janta Party releases manifesto on exam reforms at Pune protest
content: Manifesto demands ₹10,000 compensation per student in case of paper leaks, evaluation of physical exam sheets, results within a month, backup dates in case of cancellation, independent audit of exam tenders

title: Ernakulam on alert following rise in shigellosis cases in State
content: While no cases have been reported recently in the district, the threat remains, says DMO; public urged to follow food safety precautions and maintain personal hygiene

title: NTR District Collector urges public to send their wards to govt. schools
content: With modern teaching methods, improved infrastructure, and skill-development programmes, the government schools are being strengthened to compete with their private counterparts, says Collector

title: Kudumbashree district mission pilots ‘Kunjaadu’ in Vembayam for animal husbandry promotion
content: An increase in number of beneficiaries under the initiative is expected to lead to a gradual rise in livestock population

title: NC and BJP condemn Pakistan’s ‘atrocities in PoK’, seek UN intervention
content: NC chief Farooq Abdullah appealed to UNHRC to investigate the incidents and show the truth to the world of actions by Pakistan in PoK

title: Calcutta HC questions expelled TMC MLA Ritabrata Banerjee’s appointment as LoP by Speaker
content: Appearing for petitioners, senior advocate and TMC MP Kalyan Bandopadhyay argues that the Speaker’s decision ‘hit the Constitution’s basic structure’ by violating its tenth schedule that settled law governing relationship between a political party and its legislature party

title: Addressing drinking water, sewage, power issues key to Thiruvananthapuram’s growth, says Minister C.P. John
content: Transport Minister says there is a substantial gap between the demand for and supply of drinking water in the city; he adds that capacity of power substations should be upgraded to meet growing demands

title: The apex court rings its own chain
content: The Supreme Court’s reliance on suo motu cognisance has turned a once ‘rare but highly visible’ jurisdiction into a recurring instrument shaped by primetime attention and media reports; while it keeps ‘ringing’ its own chain through televised listings and supervision, the trial courts below it continue doing the work

title: India expected to be among the countries invited to join initiative to secure Strait of Hormuz: French sources
content: All issues including West Asia will be discussed when PM Modi meets President Macron, says MEA's Secretary (West) Sibi George

title: Tamil Nadu is committed to working constructively with Centre, says Vijay
content: Tamil Nadu wholeheartedly supports the nation’s aspirations and believes a developed India can be built only through empowered States, cooperative federalism, and inclusive development, the Chief Minister says in his address at the 11th Governing Council meeting of NITI Aayog

title: Congress calls rejection of Meenakshi Natarajan’s Rajya Sabha nomination ‘seat chori’
content: It says it will fight the rejection of candidate’s papers legally and politically; party plans nation-wide agitation on inflation, unemployment, NEET paper leak, CBSE OSM row among others issues

title: 'With whose permission captain took ship forward': Kin on MT Settebello route; family seeks probe
content: The family of Aditya Sharma, a deck cadet killed in the attack on MT Settebello near the Strait of Hormuz, has demanded an inquiry into the incident. Sharma was among three Indian seafarers who died when the vessel was attacked in the Gulf of Oman.

title: Stone hits Shatabdi Express near Firozabad with RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat on board
content: At present, there is no official confirmation regarding how the stone came to hit the train or who may have been responsible. Authorities are examining the circumstances surrounding the incident and gathering evidence as part of the inquiry. After the necessary checks were completed, the Shatabdi Express resumed its journey towards Delhi and reached the national capital at 10.13 pm.

title: Rain lashes Delhi-NCR after IMD issues red alert for thunderstorms, strong winds
content: The red alert, the highest level of weather warning issued by the IMD, indicates the possibility of severe weather conditions that may pose risks to life and property, requiring immediate precautionary measures. According to meteorologists, the change in weather has been driven by a cyclonic circulation over central Pakistan and adjoining regions. The system has increased atmospheric instability across northwest India, including Delhi-NCR, creating favourable conditions for thunderstorms and rainfall.

title: Omar Abdullah meets PM Modi, raises J&K statehood restoration
content: Informing about the meeting, Omar wrote on X that they also held discussions on the state of economy and the pace of development in J&K. The CM stressed the need for continued support from the Centre to accelerate economic growth, strengthen connectivity, generate employment, expand infrastructure and enhance public welfare across Jammu and Kashmir. Omar also congratulated PM Modi for completing 12 uninterrupted years in office.

title: As US expands China military list, Pentagon restrictions start June 30
content: The US Department of Defense has added 188 Chinese entities, including tech giants like Alibaba and Baidu, to its "Chinese military company" list. While this designation doesn't immediately halt business, it bars the Pentagon from direct procurement from these firms starting June 30.

title: British Indian Tory peer Rami Ranger takes UK PM Starmer to court over CBE revocation
content: Tom Hickman KC, representing Ranger, said that in revoking the CBE, the PM relied on allegations that were never put to Ranger. In addition “personal disputes” were used, even though that goes against the committee’s guidelines and the decision was taken without waiting for the outcome of a libel claim made by Sikhs for Justice. He also said it was contrary to Ranger’s Article 10 ECHR freedom of expression rights. He pointed out that forfeiture has “serious reputational consequences”.

title: J&K govt extends deadline for central assistance schemes
content: Under the revised timeline, the scheme for families displaced from PoK in 1947 and from Chhamb during the 1965 and 1971 conflicts has been extended until Sept 30, 2026. The extension will be implemented within the existing rehabilitation package outlay of Rs 2,000 crore. According to the order, MHA conveyed its approval on May 25, 2026, allowing the administration additional time to complete implementation and cover eligible beneficiaries whose cases remain pending.

title: SpaceX IPO: Could Elon Musk become world's first trillionaire? Decoding the math
content: Elon Musk may soon become the world's first trillionaire. This is due to the upcoming initial public offering of his company SpaceX. The IPO is expected to be the largest in history. If valuations hold true, Musk's personal fortune could surge past the trillion-dollar mark. This event could reshape global wealth rankings and create new millionaires.

title: Microsoft warns staff: Don't touch Claude Fable 5, lawyers are still reading fine print
content: Microsoft has blocked its own employees from using Anthropic's newly launched Claude Fable 5 inside GitHub Copilot, citing unresolved concerns over a 30-day data retention policy tied to the Mythos-class model. The Verge reports Microsoft's legal team is still reviewing the changes, even as paying Copilot and Foundry customers already have access. Other Claude models remain available internally under Zero Data Retention rules. Anthropic has not offered a workaround.

title: Missile in fuel tank: Navy's daring op to recover warhead from UAE-Kochi oil tanker
content: The Indian Navy successfully disposed of an unexploded missile warhead from a crude oil tanker, MT Olympic Life, which was en route to Kochi. The operation involved a specialist explosive ordnance disposal team that safely extracted the warhead lodged in a fuel tank, ensuring the safety of the ship and its crew.

title: Amit Shah announces potential AFSPA rollback next year
content: Home Minister Amit Shah announced that most of the Northeast may be free from the Armed Forces (Special Powers) Act by next year, citing an 80% reduction in violent incidents and AFSPA-covered areas. This progress coincides with a historic oil and mineral exploration pact between Assam and Nagaland, boosting regional development and energy self-reliance.

title: Palantir CEO’s message to tech CEO: Don’t be surprised if your employees turn against you
content: Palantir CEO Alex Karp cautions tech leaders against publicizing AI-driven job cuts, warning it could provoke backlash from employees, voters, and policymakers. He suggests such pronouncements risk fueling opposition and strengthening calls for tech regulation. Karp himself acknowledges Palantir uses AI for efficiency but plans to boost revenue without significant workforce expansion.

title: March to dominance: How victory in states is consolidating BJP in Parliament
content: The BJP, once a two-seat party, now dominates Indian politics, transforming Atal Bihari Vajpayee's prophecy into reality. Recent state victories, particularly in West Bengal, are strategically bolstering its parliamentary numbers. This focus on consolidation aims to achieve long-term legislative goals like 'One Nation, One Election' and the Uniform Civil Code.

title: Horoscope Today: Astrological predictions for June 12, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 12, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Countries protecting domestic industries, India no exception: Goyal on global trade
content: India navigates global trade challenges by fostering dialogue and partnerships, even as nations increasingly protect domestic industries like steel. Minister Piyush Goyal highlighted that despite a less effective WTO, international trade largely operates under its framework. India is actively pursuing free trade agreements to boost market access and attract significant investments, underscoring a commitment to innovation and long-term global integration.

title: Fans can watch the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony live worldwide
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 opening ceremony will feature three major celebrations across Mexico, Canada, and the United States, making it one of the biggest tournament launches in history. Artists including Shakira, Burna Boy, J Balvin, Tyla, Michael Bublé, and Nora Fatehi are set to perform. Fans in India can watch the ceremonies live on Unite8 Sports channels and Zee5. FIFA president Gianni Infantino called the event “a truly global celebration” uniting music, culture, and football.

title: Roger Federer’s luxury car collection reflects his taste for speed and style
content: Roger Federer still has eyes on him; he has been on the Mercedes-Benz radar since forever and is an embodiment of a luxury lifestyle. He has an array of beautiful sports cars, superior SUVs and luxury cars that beautifully depict his sophisticated personality. He's not a crazy car enthusiast and wants his garage to be appreciated by tennis fans for its performance and usability, which is why his collection is one of the most loved in tennis.

title: Serena Williams’ comeback hopes suffer setback after injury cuts run short
content: Serena Williams’ comeback at the 2026 HSBC Championships ended early after doubles partner Victoria Mboko withdrew because of a left knee injury. The pair had won their opening match before being forced out of the tournament. Williams, playing her first competitive event since the 2022 US Open, is expected to continue her return at the Berlin Open, according to ESPN.

title: Situation Room with a view: Trump, Epstein and Washington’s 'wag the dog' whispers
content: A new book reveals a White House meeting in July 2025 where officials debated the political fallout from the Jeffrey Epstein files. Vice President JD Vance reportedly urged transparency, warning the issue wouldn't fade. Discussions even touched on releasing documents with allegations about Trump's alleged fixation with nipples, a proposal Vance believed Trump could overcome.

title: Seeds of change: How India’s agriculture went high-output in 12 years
content: India's agriculture sector has seen remarkable growth over the past decade, fueled by increased government investment and record foodgrain production. Policies now prioritize productivity, market access, and farmer income security. This transformation, marked by doubled GVA and enhanced crop yields, aims for a resilient, technology-driven farm economy crucial for national development.

title: 'He is not just your T20 slogger': Manjrekar predicts big ODI future for Sooryavanshi
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is impressing beyond T20 cricket, showcasing adaptability in Sri Lanka's tri-series. Former India batter Sanjay Manjrekar lauded his versatile 44 off 22 balls against Afghanistan A, highlighting his potential for longer formats. Suryavanshi's innings, marked by timing and placement, earned praise for his technique, not just aggression.

title: UP woman accuses father-in-law of rape at gunpoint, husband gives triple talaq over phone
content: A Bahraich woman has accused her father-in-law of repeated rape at gunpoint, with her husband allegedly complicit and later issuing triple talaq via phone. Police have registered a case against five family members. The husband claims marital disputes and the woman's alleged affair. Investigations are ongoing, with no arrests yet.

title: Rs 370 Biryani remark: Maharashtra police books comedian More and Jangra; summons Sejal Pawar
content: Maharashtra Cyber police have filed a case against comedian Pranit More, web developer Himanshu Jangra, and others for allegedly circulating obscene content. The controversy stems from remarks made during a comedy show that allegedly glorified coercion and trivialized consent, with further offensive comments made about medical cadavers. Summons have been issued for questioning.

title: Delhi-born Nikhil Chaudhary earns maiden Australia call-up for Bangladesh T20Is
content: Delhi-born all-rounder Nikhil Chaudhary has been named in Australia's T20I squad for the Bangladesh series, marking a significant milestone as the first Indian-born male cricketer in over 60 years to achieve this. His selection follows impressive performances in the Big Bash League, and he is now in contention for his international debut.

title: TN CM Vijay meets PM Modi; urges Centre to exempt state from NEET, release pending funds
content: Addressing the council, chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Vijay said his govt would work constructively with the Union govt while firmly safeguarding TN’s interests and aspirations.

title: India retains growth edge with 6.6% growth, World Bank forecasts; cuts global outlook
content: Global growth forecasts are down to 2.5% for 2026, with the Middle East conflict and rising energy prices cited as key reasons. Despite this, India is set to remain the world's fastest-growing major economy, projected at 6.6% in fiscal year 2026-27. However, higher energy costs may moderate India's growth, while fiscal deficits are expected to widen across South Asia.

title: Hospital bills, captive patients: Why CCI’s ruling may weaken consumer protection
content: The Competition Commission of India has cleared twelve Delhi hospitals of overcharging patients. The CCI ruled that regulating healthcare prices is the government's responsibility, not the commission's. This decision comes after a decade-long investigation into allegations of inflated charges for medicines, consumables, and diagnostics. The ruling has sparked debate about consumer protection in India's vast healthcare sector.

title: 'Anything but brute force': Ashwin blown away by Sooryavanshi's 44-run blitz
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi impressed Ravichandran Ashwin with a dazzling 44 off 22 balls for India A. The 15-year-old showcased a blend of aggression and control, hitting nine boundaries. Despite missing a half-century, his performance highlighted his potential beyond T20s, earning praise from the former India spinner for his strokeplay and timing.

title: Anvay Dravid, son of legendary Rahul Dravid, named in India U-19 squad
content: Anvay Dravid, son of former India captain Rahul Dravid, has been selected for the India Under-19 squad touring Sri Lanka. The BCCI announced the squads on Thursday, with Anvay named as one of the wicketkeepers for the one-day matches. This selection follows his impressive performance in the Vinoo Mankad Trophy.

title: 10 days, a cremation and a cover-up: Woman’s final video exposes murder plot
content: A 24-year-old woman's death, initially reported as natural, has been revealed as a brutal murder. Police arrested her mother, husband, and father-in-law, who allegedly killed her and cremated the body to conceal the crime. The motive appears to stem from her desire to separate and marry another man.

title: Kerala on alert after suspected Nipah infection detected in Kozhikode, confirms health minister
content: In Kozhikode, health officials have confirmed a positive test for the Nipah virus in a patient currently requiring ventilator assistance due to severe fever. Authorities are actively tracing potential contacts, having identified 77 people, including healthcare staff and close family, who are under watch.

title: UK approves first weight loss pill as GLP-1 tablet gets green light from MHRA
content: The UK's MHRA has approved the first GLP-1 receptor agonist tablet for weight loss, offering a more convenient alternative to weekly injections. Semaglutide, branded as Wegovy, is now available in pill form for adults with obesity or those who are overweight with weight-related health conditions. Patients can transition directly from the weekly injection to the 25 mg daily tablet.

title: Veteran actor Dinyar Tirandaz passes away in Mumbai
content: Veteran actor Dinyar Tirandaz, known for his memorable roles in television shows and Bollywood films, has reportedly passed away in Mumbai. The news of his demise surfaced on social media on Thursday, leaving fans and members of the entertainment industry saddened.

title: Drunk Delhi cop enters Gurgaon society, assaults woman and threatens to rape her sister
content: A 21-year-old woman was allegedly assaulted by an intoxicated Delhi Police constable in Gurgaon's Sector 56. The accused also threatened her family and issued a rape threat to her younger sister. The constable was arrested and his bail application was dismissed. Police have added POCSO charges. CCTV footage is being examined.

title: Lalu's 79th birthday: RJD worker climbs JCB to cut cake, falls, but continues celebrating
content: During Lalu Prasad Yadav's birthday celebrations in Bihar's Vaishali district, RJD leader Kedar Yadav fell from a JCB machine while attempting to cut a cake. Despite the mishap, he continued the festivities, comparing Yadav to Nelson Mandela and calling him a leader of the poor.

title: Meenakshi Natarajan row ends in BJP’s 3-0 Rajya Sabha sweep in MP amid Congress protest
content: The BJP secured three Rajya Sabha seats in Madhya Pradesh unopposed after the Congress nominee's nomination was rejected. Despite legal challenges and protests by the Congress, the Election Commission and Supreme Court did not intervene in time, leading to the BJP's victory. This outcome concluded a politically charged battle marked by allegations of horse-trading and constitutional conspiracy.

title: How promises of jobs and a better life trapped 453 Nepalese youths in UP
content: Hundreds of Nepalese youths, lured by job promises, were rescued from a sophisticated scam in Uttar Pradesh's Kushinagar.  Victims paid hefty sums for training and placements that never materialized, facing confinement and exploitation.  Alerts to the Nepal Embassy triggered a multi-agency operation, exposing the extensive cross-border racket.

title: 'Rs 370 Biryani' row: FIR against Pranit More, others for objectionable content online
content: Stand-up comedian Pranit More faces an FIR from Maharashtra Cyber Cell for allegedly disseminating obscene content. The case stems from an audience member's controversial date story shared during More's show, which sparked debate on accountability in comedy and content creation. Both More and the audience member have since apologized.

title: ‘Human shield’ on India border: Bangladesh opposition to launch protest over alleged 'push-ins'
content: Bangladesh's Jamaat-e-Islami and opposition parties are protesting alleged Indian "push-ins" and border killings. They claim thousands were pushed back and 19 Bangladeshis killed by Indian forces. Opposition leaders demand stronger border security and criticize India's stance, emphasizing the impact on Bangladeshi lives and sovereignty.

title: Greater Noida toll plaza chaos: Miscreants break barrier, threaten employees; videos go viral
content: Alleged toll dodgers caused chaos at Greater Noida's Luharli Toll Plaza, refusing to pay fees and breaking through a barrier.  The group reportedly confronted and threatened toll plaza employees before speeding away. Police are reviewing CCTV footage to identify and apprehend the individuals involved.

title: Buyer claims Rs 1.65 crore Defender failed its speed claim; court orders full refund
content: A luxury car buyer has won a major consumer dispute against Jaguar Land Rover India. The company was ordered to refund ₹1.65 crore with interest for a defective Defender SUV. The vehicle failed to meet advertised performance, had missing features, and underwent unauthorized chassis modifications, leading to the commission's strong ruling.

title: 'Boost For Oil Exploration': Amit Shah On Agreement With Assam, Nagaland
content: Speaking at the signing of an MoU to facilitate mineral oil operations in the Assam-Nagaland boundary areas, Shah said it will open new avenues for mineral exploration in the Northeast and bring prosperity to the region.

title: Trump Names Prosecutor Jay Clayton New US Intelligence Chief
content: US President Donald Trump on Thursday named prosecutor Jay Clayton as his new intelligence chief, after a backlash from Republicans over his bid to install an inexperienced ally in the post.

title: "States Play Vital Role In National Progress": PM Modi At NITI Aayog Meet
content: The Prime Minister reiterated the government's commitment to cooperative federalism, saying states play a vital role in advancing national progress.

title: Husband's Affair, Rs 2.5 Crore Row Emerge In Punjab Doctor Suicide Case
content: A 37-year-old government doctor allegedly died by suicide, following which her husband, an ophthalmologist, was booked on charges of abetment of suicide in Punjab's Jalandhar, police said on Thursday.

title: Digvijaya Singh Alleges "Collusive Theft" In Rajya Sabha Row, BJP Seeks Action
content: At the centre of the controversy is Congress candidate Meenakshi Natarajan, whose nomination was rejected over alleged non-disclosure of a pending court matter

title: NDA Wins All 4 Rajya Sabha Seats In Andhra Unopposed
content: Three candidates of Telugu Desam Party (TDP) and one candidate of Jana Sena Party (JSP) were declared elected by the returning officer on Thursday after the period for withdrawal of nominations came to an end.

title: In A First, All 28 Chief Ministers Join NITI Aayog Governing Council Meet
content: The meeting, chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the Rashtrapati Bhavan Cultural Centre in New Delhi, was held under the theme "Inclusive Human Development for Viksit Bharat@2047."

title: Inside Shipping Containers, Education Gives Children's Dreams Flight In Thane
content: Every morning beneath a busy flyover in Thane, children walk into brightly coloured shipping containers carrying school bags, notebooks and dreams.

title: Is It Abhishek Banerjee? Trinamool MP Who Resigned On What Haunts Party
content: Prakash Chik Baraik said he owes his decision to exit from parliament and the Trinamool Congress to the mandate that the people have given in the recent election

title: Air India Ahmedabad Crash Made Elderly Couple's First Flight Their Last
content: Exactly one year ago, the sky over Ahmedabad became the stage for a nightmare that would forever alter the landscape of Indian aviation and shatter hundreds of lives in a single, catastrophic flash.

title: Andhra Man Among 3 Killed In Ship Attack, Leaves Behind 2 Young Children
content: The Palau-flagged tanker MT Settebello was operating near the Strait of Hormuz when it was struck during a US military operation. Of the 24 Indian crew members on board, 21 were rescued.

title: Putin's War In Ukraine Has Now Outlasted World War I
content: According to polls, about half of the Ukrainians believe that the war will continue longer and not end before next year. This would push the duration of the war closer to that of World War II, which ended after 6 years of fighting.

title: Company Law Tribunal Initiates Personal Insolvency Process Against Anil Ambani
content: A bench of Judicial Member Sushil Mahadeorao Kochey and Technical Member Prabhat Kumar passed the order while admitting the SBI's application filed under Section 95 of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC).

title: Centre's Ethanol-Blended Petrol Duty Exemptions To Avoid Double Taxation
content: For ethanol blends of up to 20 per cent, excise duty on the blended petrol was exempted by the Ministry of Finance. The exemption ensures that the duties already paid are not charged a second time on the blend.

title: Delhi May Make Smoke Detectors Mandatory In Every Home After Deadly Fires
content: Smoke detectors could soon become mandatory in every home in Delhi as the government considers sweeping changes to building bylaws following a series of deadly fire incidents, including the recent Hauz Rani blaze that claimed 23 lives.

title: Trinamool's Shatrughan Sinha To NDTV On Why He Wished PM Modi
content: "Whatever I say, whatever I do, I do it thoughtfully without any selfish motive," Trinamool Congress MP and actor Shatrughan Sinha said

title: Kenya Pays 50% Premium As Chinese Firm Wins Airport Deal After Adani Agreement Cancelled
content: The award to the Chinese state-owned contractor advances long-delayed plans to modernise Kenya's busiest airport, which has been operating beyond its intended capacity

title: 3 Schoolchildren Walking Home Run Over By Drunk Driver In Tamil Nadu, 1 Injured
content: Three school children lost their lives, and another sustained injuries after a speeding car, allegedly driven by a drunken driver, rammed into them in the Tiruvarur district.

content: your active subscription(s) Account subscription benefits alongside Premium Stories, Editorials, Opinions and more .
